news.title: Apple loses challenges against EU rules to curb Big Tech
news.summary:
STORY: Apple lost its challenge against landmark EU rules on Wednesday. 

The tech giant tried to refute the General Court's designation of its app stores and operating system as gatekeepers. 

Apple is now subject to obligations aimed at giving rivals more room to compete.

The EU Digital Markets Act (DMA) sets out a list of rules for Big Tech with the threat of fines of as much as 10% of global annual turnover.

It has triggered legal challenges by Apple, Meta and ByteDance since it took effect in May 2023.

The ruling by the Luxembourg-based General Court will strengthen the position of EU antitrust regulators.

That’s as they try to make space for rivals and give Europeans more choice.

Apple first took its protests to the Court in 2024.

It again criticized the DMA on Wednesday, saying its mandate “goes beyond what is lawful and proportionate”. 

Apple can appeal on matters of law to the Court of Justice of the European Union, Europe's highest.
